governance.permitted_use: Research and benchmarking; private use only.
governance.redistribution_rights: Official ECOSTRESS page requests citation and states copyright/all rights reserved; converted matrices are private/internal until redistribution rights are clarified.

identity.description: ECOSTRESS vegetation vswir axis 4d4366d1. v2.0 standardized NIRS package: 1 spectral source(s), 3 declared target(s). Auto-generated from dataset_card.json (verify before publication).

diagnostics.interpretation: Rupture aux jonctions de détecteurs, calibration locale ou sonde différente.
diagnostics.label: Splice / raccord détecteurs

diagnostics.interpretation: Artefacts numériques ou traitement spectral incorrect.
diagnostics.label: Erreur interpolation / rééchantillonnage

diagnostics.interpretation: Combinaison possible changement de sonde + splice, amplifiée par géométrie, fond ou calibration.
diagnostics.label: Signature VERA25-like

diagnostics.interpretation: Décalage systématique entre campagnes, instruments ou référence blanche.
diagnostics.label: Erreur calibration / référence blanche

diagnostics.interpretation: Effet systématique du support, blanc/noir, transflectance ou environnement de mesure.
diagnostics.label: Fond différent

diagnostics.interpretation: Mélange de campagnes, opérateurs, lots, setups ou sous-populations spectrales.
diagnostics.label: Dataset multi-régimes

diagnostics.interpretation: Modification de l'illumination, collecte, angle ou distance sonde-échantillon.
diagnostics.label: Différence de sonde / géométrie

diagnostics.interpretation: Variété, espèce, lot ou condition différente mais physiquement plausible.
diagnostics.label: Spectre hors domaine valide

top_classes.name: Samples were collected at the Huntington Garden in San Marino California as part of a JPL Subcontract studying HyTES imagery. Samples were taken to JPL and processed within 48 hours of collection. The same leaves were processed in the Nicolet and then measured using the ASD.

Samples were collected as part of the HyspIRI Airborne Campaign proposal titled: HyspIRI discrimination of plant species and functional types along a strong environmental temperature gradient. The same materials were processed in the Nicolet and then measured using the ASD.
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
Copyright California Institute of Technology / JPL, all rights reserved
